LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF JOHN STATHAKIS, KRISTOPHER STATHAKIS' MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSE TO INTERROGATORIES AND FOR MONETARY SANCTION AGAINST DEFENDANTS IN AMOUNT OF 7,860.00. THE TRIAL REMAINS ON 1/14/19. PLAINTIFF JOHN STATHAKIS' MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO INTERROGATORIES AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IS GRANTED IN PART. DEFENDANTS ARE REQUIRED TO PROVIDE FURTHER RESPONSES AS STATED IN THE REPORT OF JUDGE PRO TEM PAUL RENNE BY NEXT WEDNESDAY, 1/16/19 AND MUST BE RECEIVED BY MR. KING. DEFENDANTS ARE REQUIRED TO PAY $2500 TO MR. STATHAKIS DUE BY THE END OF THE TRIAL. THE COURT ADOPTS IN FULL THE RECOMMENDATIONS AND REASONS FOR THOSE RECOMMENDATIONS IN THE REPORT OF MR. RENNE. (AS FULLY REPORTED IN THE REPORTER'S TRANSCRIPT) COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF, MR. KING TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT IS VERBATIM WITH THE COURT'S RULING IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Jan-15-2019 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On May-02-23 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice And Motion For Trial Preference; Dec. Of William E. King
Off Calendar Jan-10-2019 Text Ruling

Notice And Motion To Compel Further Response To Interrogatories; Declaration Of William E. King; And For Monetary Sanction Against Defendants In Amount Of 7,860.00
Off Calendar Jan-09-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
William E. King, Esq. 707-331-2834 for Plaintiff.
Bradley Kass, Esq. 650-579-0612 of Kass & Kass for Defendant Nicholas Botaitis through Court Call.

RULING - A R G U E D. Defendant argues. The Court passes the case to give copies of Judge Pro Tem Paul Renne's report to the parties. The matter is recalled at 10:13 am. Defendant opposes sanctions of $2500. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PLAINTIFF JOHN STATHAKIS, KRISTOPHER STATHAKIS' Motion To Compel Further Response To Interrogatories And For Monetary Sanction Against Defendants In Amount Of 7,860.00.

The trial remains on 1/14/19. Plaintiff John Stathakis' motion to compel further responses to interrogatories and for monetary sanctions is granted in part. Defendants are required to provide further responses as stated in the report of Judge Pro Tem Paul Renne by next Wednesday, 1/16/19 and must be received by Mr. King. Defendants are required to pay $2500 to Mr. Stathakis due by the end of the trial. The court adopts in full the recommendations and reasons for those recommendations in the report of Mr. Renne. (as fully reported in the Reporter's transcript)

Counsel for Plaintiff, Mr. King to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/HK)

Atty. BRADLEY M. KASS, ESQ. of Kass & Kass (Tel: 650-579-0612) via CourtCall for defendant Nichjolas D. Botatitis.

Plaintiff stipulates to the pro tem judge. Defendant does not stipulate to the pro tem judge. The matter is addressed by the parties and the pro tem judge. The hearing on Plaintiffs' motion to compel further responses to certain interrogatories directed to plaintiffs is continued to January 9, 2019 at 9:30 a.m. in Dept. 302, Law & Motion.
